title: rosa glauca subvar. rigida
intro: Rosa glauca subvar. rigida, also known as the alpine rose or prickly rose, is a type of shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe. Its leaves and stems have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Rosa glauca subvar. rigida is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its effectiveness and safety may vary depending on the specific preparation and individual needs.
